intro: Medranoa palmeri, also known as Palmer's medranoa, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the island of Tahiti in French Polynesia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include treating fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Medranoa palmeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional Polynesian medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, although modern research on its efficacy is limited.
